one_time_description: COVID-19 related expenditures of $6,287,945 with matching revenue recognized in year
economic_notes: Nation holds substantial investment portfolio of $91.1M through CIBC Private Wealth Management and RBC Wealth Management including Land Claim and Agricultural Benefit trust funds. Nation owns/controls Atikameg Construction and Oilfield Maintenance Corporation, Atikameg Timber Limited Partnership, Atikameg Timber Ltd., Kee Tas Kee Now Sawmill Limited Partnership, Kee Tas Kee Now Sawmill Ltd., Whitefish Lake First Nation Child and Family Services, and Whitefish Lake Band Development Corporation.
notes: Fiscal year is 2021 not 2024. Nation holds $91.1M in trust investment portfolios (Land Claim and Agricultural Benefit funds) which significantly inflates financial assets. The Statement of Change in Net Financial Assets shows excess of revenues over expenses of $16,428,796 which includes trust fund investment gains, whereas the operating Statement of Financial Activities shows $2,940,002 surplus - these differ due to trust fund earnings included in net asset calculation. COVID-19 expenditures of $6,287,945 are a significant one-time program. KTC/ISC revenue of $14,620,384 flows through Kee Tas Kee Now Tribal Council - ISC-specific amount not separately disclosed. Seven controlled entities consolidated by equity method. No schedule of remuneration provided. Line of credit of $895,000 included in current liabilities. Prior year accumulated surplus restated. Auditor is Doyle & Company, Edmonton AB, engagement partner Scott Mockford CPA CA.
